provenance: Derived from the KORA food-hall prospects list (koraprospectsFULL.csv). Staged here are the leads that are (a) NOT already in the venues catalogue and (b) located in a Bali district. Verification level varies and is preserved per venue; nothing is published.
piiPolicy: Contact-person names, phone/WA numbers, emails and links are omitted or redacted from free-text; the internal per-lead verification log was not imported (AGENTS.md guardrails 12 and 14).

selection.excludedJakartaSingaporeChains: held for a specific-outlet decision (uncertain/multi-outlet Bali presence): Toko Kopi Tuku, Sate Khas Senayan, Bakso Boedjangan, Gildak, Dailybox, Bittersweet, Fore, Hangry

notes: These are venue CANDIDATES, not published venues. verifiedAt stays null until an official-source check + operator/owner verification.
'leadVerificationStatus' is the tracker's own status and is NOT Other Bali publication verification.
districtNeedsConfirmation=true means the tracker only gave 'Other Bali' as the area; a specific district must be set before promotion.
Kept outside data/data-ops/batches/ on purpose: no menu/action content, must not enter the batch compiler until promoted.

venues.name: Alma Tapas Bar
venues.currentLocations: 2: Berawa (Jl. Pantai Berawa 90, Canggu) and Bingin, Uluwatu (Jl. Pantai Bingin 4)
reviewNotes: No public email/phone found (site blocked direct fetch, bookings run through Chope); founder name not publicly verifiable.
venues.address: Jl. Pantai Berawa No.89C, Canggu, Kec. Kuta Utara, Kabupaten Badung, Bali 80361
venues.addressResearchNotes: Canggu/Berawa branch; also Bingin/Uluwatu branch.

editorial.why_its_here: A neighbourhood Spanish tapas bar in Berawa built around shared plates, wine, sangria and vermouth in a rustic, Mediterranean-styled room. The menu spans land, sea and garden small plates meant to be ordered across the table.
editorial.best_for: shared plates with wine; casual date over tapas; small group grazing; unhurried evening in canggu
editorial.not_for: anyone wanting a fast solo bite or a single large main course
editorial.what_to_order: patatas bravas; chorizo al vino; pulpo a la gallega (octopus); tortilla española; grilled prawns; sangria or vermouth

venues.name: Ayam Betutu Khas Gilimanuk
venues.cuisine: Balinese — ayam betutu, plecing, sambal matah
venues.currentLocations: Multi-branch chain across South Bali, incl. Renon/Denpasar (Jl. Merdeka No. 5) and Tuban/Kuta (Jl. Raya Tuban No. 2X); brand origin Gilimanuk bus station, 1978
reviewNotes: Founder was 'Men Tempeh' (1978); the branch brand was built out by a family member known publicly as 'Oka' (per Will Meyrick's article) but no full legal name is published — verify current owner before naming. Multiple published branch phones; [number removed] (Renon) is mobile.
venues.address: Jl. Merdeka No. 88, Renon, Denpasar Timur, Kota Denpasar, Bali 80235
venues.addressResearchNotes: betutugilimanuk brand Renon/Merdeka branch; do not confuse with Ayam Betutu Ibu Nia (Jl. Merdeka No.1).

editorial.why_its_here: A long-running Denpasar specialist in ayam betutu, the Gilimanuk-style chicken slow-cooked in Balinese spice paste until it falls off the bone. It is a focused, no-frills local kitchen where the betutu and the sambals are the whole point.
editorial.best_for: trying a genuine balinese classic; a spice-forward local lunch; eating where residents eat away from the tourist strip
editorial.not_for: people avoiding chilli or wanting mild flavours; anyone after a western menu, cocktails or a scenic view
editorial.what_to_order: ayam betutu; nasi campur ayam betutu; plecing kangkung; sambal matah

venues.name: Bebek Bengil
venues.currentLocations: Original Ubud (Jl. Hanoman, since 1990) + Nusa Dua (The Bay), Jimbaran, and Jakarta outlets at BSD, Plaza Indonesia, Aloha PIK2, Kelapa [name removed]ng
reviewNotes: Corporate/leasing decision-maker not published; om.ubud@ address is the outlet reservation email - ask to be routed to business development.
venues.address: Jl. Hanoman, Padang Tegal, Ubud, Gianyar Regency, Bali 80571, Indonesia
venues.addressResearchNotes: Ubud flagship, Jl. Hanoman.

editorial.why_its_here: The Ubud restaurant that popularised Balinese-style crispy duck, open since 1990, set among rice-paddy garden pavilions. The duck is steamed in Indonesian spices then deep-fried until the skin crackles while the meat stays tender.
editorial.best_for: first taste of balinese crispy duck; rice-paddy garden dinner; groups sharing regional dishes; visitors near monkey forest
editorial.not_for: diners who want a quiet hidden local warung; vegetarians, since the menu centres on duck and meat
editorial.what_to_order: crispy duck (bebek bengil); duck satay; smoked duck (order a day ahead)

venues.name: BO$$MAN
venues.currentLocations: 2 locations: Seminyak (Jl. Kayu Cendana No.8B) and Canggu (Jl. Pantai Batu Bolong No.69); same ownership as Sisterfields (Project Black)
reviewNotes: No named individual owner publicly verifiable; only group social email published.
venues.address: Jl. Kayu Cendana No. 8B, Seminyak, Kec. Kuta Utara, Kabupaten Badung, Bali 80361
venues.addressResearchNotes: Seminyak flagship, Jl. Kayu Cendana.

editorial.why_its_here: A Seminyak burger joint built on Australian-beef smash and gourmet patties with house-made sauces and freshly baked buns. It runs late, serving until roughly 5am, which makes it a reliable post-night-out stop.
editorial.best_for: late-night cravings; casual meat-forward meals; after a night out; quick group bite
editorial.not_for: a quiet, refined sit-down dinner; anyone avoiding meat-heavy fare
editorial.what_to_order: Original Gangster; Al Capone; Lucky Pig; smash burger; chicken sando

venues.name: Dapur Bali Mula
venues.cuisine: Balinese heritage / Bali Mula (wood-fire, clay-stove North Bali cooking, base genep)
venues.currentLocations: 1 location: Dapur Bali Mula, Les village, Tejakula, Buleleng (North Bali)
reviewNotes: No published email/phone found (reservations via Instagram DM only); unclear if he would leave/duplicate the village model — pitch may need a mentored-team format; donation-based pricing may need adapting to commercial rent + revenue share.
Located in Les village, North Bali (Buleleng) — outside the guide's districts.
venues.address: Desa Les, Tejakula, Buleleng Regency, Bali 81173, Indonesia
venues.addressResearchNotes: Les village, Tejakula, North Bali (Buleleng) — OUTSIDE guide districts; district null; cannot publish.

venues.name: Drifter Surf Shop & Cafe
venues.currentLocations: 2 locations: Uluwatu flagship shop/cafe/gallery (Jl. Labuansait No. 52, since 2008-09) and Seminyak shop
reviewNotes: Confirm whether the cafe operates as a separable F&B unit from the retail business.
venues.address: Jl. Labuansait No.52, Pecatu, Kec. Kuta Sel., Kabupaten Badung, Bali 80361
venues.addressResearchNotes: Uluwatu (Pecatu), Jl. Labuansait.

editorial.why_its_here: A healthy all-day cafe built inside Drifter's surf-lifestyle store, open since 2008 and set in an open-air Javanese limasan with rotating art. The kitchen leans clean and local, from plant-based to pescatarian, alongside specialty coffee from Sumatra.
editorial.best_for: post-surf refuel; healthy breakfast; specialty coffee; browsing surf gear over a long lunch
editorial.not_for: travellers wanting a traditional Balinese warung experience; anyone after a quiet fine-dining setting rather than a busy surf-shop cafe
editorial.what_to_order: smoothie bowls; brekkie burrito; toasts; cold-pressed juices; Mandailing coffee

venues.name: Expat. Roasters
venues.currentLocations: Roastery/HQ in Kerobokan-Denpasar area; cafes at Jl. Petitenget No.1A and Beachwalk Kuta; outlets and wholesale across Bali, Jakarta, Surabaya, Makassar
reviewNotes: No public email found (contact page is a web form, page blocked for direct fetch); some guides mention an Ubud cafe - verify whether they currently operate in Ubud before pitching exclusivity.
venues.address: Jl. Petitenget No. 1A, Kerobokan Kelod, Kec. Kuta Utara, Kabupaten Badung, Bali 80361
venues.addressResearchNotes: Petitenget/Seminyak flagship brew bar.

editorial.why_its_here: A specialty coffee roastery and cafe that sources and roasts its own beans, working directly with Indonesian producers including farms near the roastery. Single-origin Indonesian brews like Bali Kintamani sit alongside a small menu of light bites.
editorial.best_for: serious coffee lovers; single-origin tasting; laptop-friendly morning work; buying beans and brew gear
editorial.not_for: a full sit-down meal; a lively bar or nightlife scene; anyone after a beach or view setting
editorial.what_to_order: single-origin Bali Kintamani; espresso and macchiato; tuna toastie; ham and cheese toastie

venues.name: Gusto Gelato & Caffe
venues.currentLocations: 3 locations: Kerobokan (Jl. Mertanadi No.46B, flagship), Seminyak, and Renon/Denpasar (Jl. Merdeka No.14, opened 2020)
reviewNotes: Founder/owner name not publicly verifiable from sources found; published email is the Renon branch Gmail - confirm HQ contact.
venues.address: Jl. Mertanadi No.46B, Kerobokan Kelod, Kec. Kuta Utara, Kabupaten Badung, Bali 80361, Indonesia
venues.addressResearchNotes: Original Jl. Mertanadi, Kerobokan (near Seminyak).

editorial.why_its_here: An artisan gelateria and caffe turning out house-made gelato in a wide flavour range, from classic fior di latte to local twists like lemongrass, ginger and sesame. It also pours proper coffee in a calm sit-down space.
editorial.best_for: afternoon dessert stop; cooling off in the heat; family treat with kids; coffee-and-gelato pause
editorial.not_for: anyone looking for a full sit-down meal or dinner service
editorial.what_to_order: fior di latte; gianduia; pistachio; seasonal local flavours (lemongrass, ginger, sesame); espresso alongside

venues.name: HOME by Chef Wayan
venues.cuisine: Modern Indonesian / Balinese, ocean-inspired (seafood, mother's-recipe Balinese, vegan menu)
venues.currentLocations: 1 location: HOME by Chef [name removed], Jl. Pantai Pererenan No.92, Pererenan (Canggu side)
reviewNotes: No published email/WhatsApp number captured from public pages seen (reservations stated as via WhatsApp/Instagram; number not shown in results). Confirm his consulting workload allows a second site.
venues.address: Jl. Pantai Pererenan No. 92, Pererenan, Kec. Mengwi, Kabupaten Badung, Bali 80351
venues.addressResearchNotes: Pererenan (Canggu). Real place link.

editorial.why_its_here: Modern Balinese cooking from Chef Wayan Kresna Yasa in a seaside Pererenan setting, reworking family and island recipes with contemporary technique. The kitchen is spice- and produce-driven and keeps a full vegan section alongside its seafood and traditional dishes.
editorial.best_for: a considered balinese dinner; introducing someone to indonesian flavours done well; a table mixing meat, seafood and vegan diners
editorial.not_for: travellers wanting a cheap warung price point; anyone after generic western comfort food
editorial.what_to_order: bebek betutu; babi guling; ayam srosop; braised lettuce with tempe crumble

venues.name: Hungry Bird Coffee Roaster
venues.currentLocations: 1 cafe/roastery: Jl. Raya Semat / Segara Perancak 86, Tibubeneng, Canggu (roasting since 2013); wholesale supply to cafes across Bali and Indonesia
reviewNotes: Official website/email not found in search results; owner name (roaster/founder) not publicly verified. Confirm via IG DM.
venues.address: Jl. Segara Perancak No.86, Tibubeneng, Kuta Utara, Badung Regency, Bali 80361, Indonesia
venues.addressResearchNotes: Tibubeneng/Canggu; street naming varies.

editorial.why_its_here: A specialty coffee roastery that has roasted its own beans since 2013, working directly with local farms, paired with an all-day brunch kitchen. The house blends and single origins are the reason to come, with a laptop-friendly room to linger in.
editorial.best_for: coffee-led brunch; laptop work and long stays; post-surf breakfast; buying house-roasted beans
editorial.not_for: late-night plans, as the kitchen closes in the afternoon; a big shared sit-down dinner
editorial.what_to_order: house-roasted espresso and filter coffee; banana pancakes; naughty monkey bowl

venues.name: Kilig Bali
venues.cuisine: Filipino (adobo, lechon belly sisig, kinilaw, halo-halo)
venues.currentLocations: 1 location: Kilig Filipino Warung, Jl. Raya Goa Gajah 94, Peliatan, Ubud; plus Kilig Bali Catering / private-chef services
reviewNotes: Confirm whether he prefers WhatsApp or email for business enquiries; catering arm capacity vs. second fixed location.
venues.address: Jl. Raya Goa Gajah No.94, Peliatan, Ubud, Kec. Ubud, Kabupaten Gianyar, Bali 80582
venues.addressResearchNotes: Jl. Raya Goa Gajah near Goa Gajah; closed Mon & Tue.

editorial.why_its_here: A Filipino warung in Peliatan set in a bamboo hut with rice-field views, cooking home-style Filipino comfort food. It stands out as a dedicated Filipino kitchen in an area dominated by Balinese and Western menus.
editorial.best_for: filipino comfort food; calm rice-field setting; shared table meals; family dinner
editorial.not_for: a quick central-Ubud stop given the Peliatan location; anyone avoiding rich, pork-forward dishes
editorial.what_to_order: adobo; sisig; lechon belly roll; sinigang; kinilaw; halo-halo; leche flan

venues.name: Kurasu
venues.cuisine: Japanese specialty coffee, ceremonial matcha, hojicha drinks
venues.currentLocations: Kyoto (multiple stands/roastery), plus international outposts; Bali presence was the Tanah Gajah Ubud pop-up (Nov 2025 – 8 May 2026)
reviewNotes: No published business-development email verified ([link removed] site has contact channels — verify before emailing); whether the Bali entity/partner from the Tanah Gajah collaboration continues post-pop-up. | Ubud pop-up stated to end 8 May 2026 — re-verify current presence before outreach.
Ubud presence is an unconfirmed pop-up (Tanah Gajah, stated to end ~May 2026); not central Ubud.
venues.address: Tanah Gajah Resort, Jl. Raya Goa Gajah, Kemenuh, Ubud area, Gianyar, Bali
venues.addressResearchNotes: PERMANENCE UNCONFIRMED — pop-up inside Tanah Gajah resort, stated to run until ~8 May 2026; not central Ubud (Kemenuh/Sukawati). Do NOT publish; needs_verification.

venues.name: Melting Wok Warung
venues.cuisine: French-Laotian / Southeast Asian fusion (curries, wok, crepes)
venues.currentLocations: 2 in Ubud: Melting Wok Warung (Jl. Gootama, 8 tables) and La Brasserie by Melting Wok
reviewNotes: Owners' surnames and any email/phone not publicly published - reach via Instagram DM or in person at the warung.
venues.address: Jl. Gootama No.13, Ubud, Kec. Ubud, Gianyar Regency, Bali 80571, Indonesia
venues.addressResearchNotes: Jl. Gootama.

editorial.why_its_here: A tiny eight-table warung run by a French-Laotian couple, serving fragrant coconut curries, stir-fries and crepes off a short blackboard menu that changes daily. The Laotian herbs and French dessert touches make it distinct from standard Ubud warungs.
editorial.best_for: an intimate dinner; herb-forward coconut curries; a short curated menu; foodies after fusion flavour
editorial.not_for: large groups; walk-ins without a booking; anyone needing a wide menu or fast turnover
editorial.what_to_order: tempeh coconut curry with rice noodles; coconut curries; dumplings; chicken crepe; coco crepe with brown sugar and coconut cream

venues.name: Menega Cafe
venues.currentLocations: Menega Cafe, Jl. Four Seasons, Muaya Beach, Jimbaran (flagship); also manages sister venue Warung Sakura Seafood, Jimbaran
reviewNotes: No official website or verified Instagram handle found; owner name not published. Published number is a landline, so first approach should be a visit or call, not WhatsApp. Risk to probe: whether the brand works without the beachfront setting.
venues.address: Muaya Beach (Pantai Muaya), Jimbaran, Kec. Kuta Selatan, Kabupaten Badung, Bali 80361
venues.addressResearchNotes: Muaya Beach, Jimbaran; informal street addressing; district resolved.

editorial.why_its_here: A long-running Jimbaran Bay seafood cafe where you pick fresh seafood by weight from the display and it is grilled over coconut-husk fires. Tables sit directly on Muaya Beach for toes-in-the-sand dining at sunset.
editorial.best_for: beachfront seafood dinner; sunset over the bay; groups sharing a seafood spread; special evening out
editorial.not_for: budget quick bites; vegetarians seeking variety; anyone wanting a quiet or air-conditioned room
editorial.what_to_order: grilled red snapper; butter prawns; crab in Padang sauce; grilled lobster

venues.name: Motel Mexicola
venues.currentLocations: 2 open: Seminyak (Jl. Kayu Jati No.9X, est. 2013) and Canggu; third venue announced for Uluwatu in 2026
reviewNotes: Founder/owner name not publicly verifiable in sources found; their signature format is large-venue party dining - needs framing as a compact taqueria.
venues.address: Jl. Kayu Jati No.9, Petitenget, Seminyak, Kuta, Badung Regency, Bali 80361, Indonesia
venues.addressResearchNotes: Flagship Jl. Kayu Jati, Petitenget.

editorial.why_its_here: A high-energy Mexican cantina in Petitenget known for sharing-style street food, a big tequila and mezcal list, and a colourful, music-driven room that turns into a party as the night goes on. Food and drinks are built for a lively group table.
editorial.best_for: group night out; birthdays and celebrations; loud and social dinner; cocktails and dancing
editorial.not_for: anyone after a quiet, low-key or romantic meal
editorial.what_to_order: pork al pastor tacos; beer-battered fish tacos; ceviche; chargrilled corn; margaritas; mezcal cocktails

venues.name: Nalu Bowls
venues.currentLocations: 5 Bali locations (Seminyak/Shelter at Jl. Drupadi 1 No.2A, Kuta, Canggu, Berawa, Uluwatu) plus franchises in Jakarta and Portugal
reviewNotes: Founder name not publicly verifiable from sources found; confirm which entity handles new-location decisions (Bali HQ vs franchisor).
venues.address: Jl. Drupadi 1 No.2A, Seminyak, Kec. Kuta, Kabupaten Badung, Bali 80361
venues.addressResearchNotes: Seminyak original, Jl. Drupadi; multiple outlets exist.

editorial.why_its_here: A compact smoothie-bowl specialist whose bowls are each named after a famous surf break and built from tropical fruit, house granola and simple toppings. It is a grab-and-go healthy breakfast spot rather than a sit-down cafe.
editorial.best_for: a fast healthy breakfast; a post-beach refuel; a light fruit-based bite on the go
editorial.not_for: anyone wanting a full cooked brunch, a long laptop session or a large dining room
editorial.what_to_order: uluwatu bowl; pina colada bowl; strawberry banana smoothie

venues.name: Saigon Street
venues.currentLocations: 1 Bali venue (Jl. Petitenget Raya No.77X, since 2015); previously expanded the concept to Sydney as Saigon Lane
reviewNotes: [link removed] domain now serves gambling spam (likely lapsed) so info@ email may be dead - verify current operation on-site or via Facebook before outreach; directory listings (Qraved, FirstTable) still show it open as of 2026.
venues.address: Jl. Petitenget No. 77X, Kerobokan, Kec. Kuta Utara, Kabupaten Badung, Bali 80361
venues.addressResearchNotes: Petitenget; still operating per 2026 listings; use FB page, .com domain lapsed.

editorial.why_its_here: A design-forward Vietnamese restaurant in the Petitenget dining strip, serving modern takes on classic and regional dishes alongside a late cocktail bar. Plates are built for sharing in a vivid, colourful interior.
editorial.best_for: shared plates with cocktails; lively date night; group dinner out; a design-led dinner setting
editorial.not_for: a quiet, low-key intimate meal; travellers after the cheapest street-food price point
editorial.what_to_order: pho bo with wagyu beef; steamed bao buns; dumplings; fresh rolls and wraps

venues.name: Sayuri Healing Food
venues.currentLocations: 1 cafe + academy, Peliatan, Ubud (since 2016)
reviewNotes: Email address was redacted in search results - confirm via [link removed] or Linktree; WhatsApp from their published Linktree/site snippet.

venues.addressResearchNotes: Jl. Sukma Kesuma, Peliatan.

editorial.why_its_here: A raw-vegan and plant-based cafe in central Ubud focused on living-food cooking, cold-pressed juices and a rotating case of raw desserts, with an attached plant-based academy. The kitchen leans into raw preparation rather than standard cooked vegan fare.
editorial.best_for: plant-based and raw eating; relaxed daytime meals; wellness-minded visitors; long cafe sits
editorial.not_for: committed meat-eaters; anyone wanting a fast, familiar hearty meal
editorial.what_to_order: raw lasagna; Life is a Blessing cake; smoothie bowls; raw cheesecake; cold-pressed juices

venues.name: Secret Spot
venues.cuisine: 100% plant-based cafe (vegan breakfast-dinner, vegan croissants, house-roasted coffee)
venues.currentLocations: 2: Berawa (opened 2017) and Canggu (Jl. Tanah Barak 7, opened 2019)
reviewNotes: No official website found (only SEO clone sites carrying unverified phone numbers - do not use those); owner name not publicly verifiable. Verify best IG handle (@secretspot.bali vs @secretspotbali) before DM.

venues.addressResearchNotes: Jl. Tanah Barak; Berawa branch also exists.

editorial.why_its_here: A fully plant-based cafe serving vegan breakfast through dinner, with vegan croissants, bowls, curries and ramen, plus coffee roasted weekly on the island. Extras like Monday pasta nights with live jazz and a chicory coffee alternative round out the character.
editorial.best_for: vegan breakfast and brunch; plant-based croissants and pastries; island-roasted coffee; relaxed all-day dining
editorial.not_for: diners set on meat or seafood dishes; anyone wanting a fast grab-and-go stop
editorial.what_to_order: vegan croissants; pesto and cheeze croissant; pancakes; full English breakfast; vegan bowls and curries; chicory coffee

venues.addressResearchNotes: Berawa/Canggu branch.

editorial.why_its_here: A sushi-train restaurant on Jl. Pantai Berawa serving classic and creative sushi and maki plus an izakaya-style a la carte range. The menu extends to poke bowls, donburi, sushi burritos and sushi donuts, with gluten-free, vegan and vegetarian options.
editorial.best_for: casual sushi with friends; interactive sushi-train dining; solo diners; mixed dietary groups
editorial.not_for: a formal or high-end omakase occasion; a quiet intimate dinner; anyone wanting a sea view
editorial.what_to_order: sushi-train plates; maki rolls; poke bowls; donburi; sushi burrito

editorial.why_its_here: A relaxed, vegetarian-leaning cafe set in a leafy open-air space near Bingin, serving wholesome all-day plates and hosting weekly live-music nights. It doubles as a social hub with regular Thursday music and a fitness/yoga community on site.
editorial.best_for: post-surf brunch near bingin; healthy plant-forward meal; laid-back live-music night; casual group hang
editorial.not_for: anyone wanting a formal, fine-dining or meat-forward dinner
editorial.what_to_order: the soul bowl; corn fritters; quesadillas; fresh juices and smoothies; vegan cakes and desserts

editorial.why_its_here: A Bondi-inspired all-day brunch cafe on Batu Bolong with specialty coffee and a large plant-based selection. It leans to healthy breakfast staples, bowls and toasts in a relaxed Canggu setting.
editorial.best_for: a slow brunch after the beach; specialty coffee and a laptop; plant-based and healthy breakfast eaters
editorial.not_for: anyone seeking a quiet non-touristy local spot or a traditional balinese meal
editorial.what_to_order: dragon fruit smoothie bowl; coconut pancakes; avocado on toast with poached eggs; matcha latte

editorial.why_its_here: A coconut-focused dessert and drinks shop where everything is built around fresh coconut, including vegan coconut ice cream served in the shell with roasted coconut curls. A quick, cooling stop in central Ubud.
editorial.best_for: a cooling coconut treat while walking ubud; vegan dessert; quick sweet break; fresh coconut drinks
editorial.not_for: a full sit-down meal or savoury dinner; anyone avoiding coconut
editorial.what_to_order: coconut ice cream in the shell; coconut affogato; fresh coconut water

venues.cuisine: French pâtisserie & plated desserts with Indonesian flavours

editorial.why_its_here: A French patisserie inside Kanvaz resort from Bordeaux-trained pastry chef Vincent Nigita, pairing classical French technique with Indonesian flavours. Classic pastries sit alongside a weekend tea-room experience of plated desserts.
editorial.best_for: afternoon pastry and tea; a sweet treat with care; a calm indulgent pause; a special sweet moment
editorial.not_for: a full savoury meal or a budget-casual bite; those after a quick grab-and-go snack
editorial.what_to_order: Paris-Brest; Saint Honore; chocolate eclair; Japanese fan creations; weekend tea-room dessert experience

venues.name: Warung Biah Biah
venues.currentLocations: 2 in Ubud: Warung Biah Biah (Jl. Goutama 13, since 2002) and Biahbiah+ Balinese Food & Dining
reviewNotes: Owner/founder name not published - ask via WhatsApp; email sourced from a public directory snippet, verify on first contact.
venues.address: Jl. Goutama Sel. No.13, Ubud, Kecamatan Ubud, Kabupaten Gianyar, Bali 80571, Indonesia
venues.addressResearchNotes: Jl. Goutama, Ubud. Real place URL.

editorial.why_its_here: A long-running home-style Balinese warung open since 2002, serving authentic nasi campur and local classics at warung prices. Known for its house chili sauce and a spread of Balinese sides.
editorial.best_for: authentic Balinese food; budget local dining; nasi campur; a casual central Ubud meal
editorial.not_for: diners wanting a quiet or upscale setting; groups needing lots of space during busy peak hours
editorial.what_to_order: nasi campur; balinese tapas; mie goreng; soto ayam; nasi goreng

venues.name: Warung Wardani
venues.cuisine: Balinese — nasi campur Bali, soto, gado-gado
venues.currentLocations: Original at Jl. Yudistira No. 2, Denpasar (since the 1980s); branch at Jl. Waringin No. 3, Kuta/Tuban; branch at Transmart Cempaka Putih, Jakarta
reviewNotes: Current owner/family decision-maker name not publicly documented. Catering WhatsApp [number removed] also published on Instagram per search results.
venues.address: Jl. Yudistira No.2, Dangin Puri Kauh, Kec. Denpasar Utara, Kota Denpasar, Bali 80232
venues.addressResearchNotes: Denpasar original, Jl. Yudistira No.2; Kuta branch exists.

editorial.why_its_here: A decades-old Denpasar warung known for nasi campur Bali, plated with items such as satay, dendeng and vegetables over rice. It is a longtime local institution also recognised for serving halal Balinese food, open daytime only.
editorial.best_for: authentic Balinese nasi campur; quick local lunch; halal Balinese food; solo or budget dining
editorial.not_for: dinner or late-night meals; alcohol and a bar scene; an upscale or scenic setting
editorial.what_to_order: nasi campur Bali; nasi kare; gado-gado; soto ayam; soto daging

addressResearch.method: parallel web research (official sites, Google Maps, Tripadvisor/Chope/Foursquare directories)
